summary: Implemented and matched more of src/melee/gm/gmmain_lib around VS mode, nametag, persistent name data, fighter/name statistics reset, and a final gmMainLib_8015FA34 match tweak. The decomp-dev bot reported +1284 matched code bytes and +8 matched data bytes, with new full matches for gmMainLib_8015F260, InitializePersistentNameData, .sdata2, and gmMainLib_8015FA34, plus partial improvements for gmMainLib_8015DBF4, gmMainLib_8015F600, gmMainLib_8015EA80, and gmMainLib_8015F150.
key_files: src/melee/gm/gmmain_lib.c - added implementations for gmMainLib_8015DBF4, gmMainLib_8015EA80, InitializePersistentNameData, gmMainLib_8015F150, gmMainLib_8015F260, gmMainLib_8015F600, and matching edits for gmMainLib_8015FA34
src/melee/gm/gmmain_lib.h - replaced several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes with concrete s32/void signatures and changed gmMainLib_8015FA34 argument type to s32
what_changed: Added includes for placeholder.h, melee/if/textlib.h, melee/mn/mnname.h, and melee/ty/tylist.h to support the new gmmain_lib implementations.

Implemented gmMainLib_8015DBF4(s32): uses local ADJ_NAMETAG_78 and ADJ_VMD macros to update u8 nametag/player references across gmMainLib_804D3EE0 fields, gm_80497618, multiple VsModeData slots, and GameRules fields, then returns arg0.
Implemented gmMainLib_8015EA80(void): resets available name-related slots from gmMainLib_8015CE44(i, 0x78) to 5 and sets handicap to 9 across selected VsModeData ranges.

Implemented InitializePersistentNameData(s32): locates a NameTagData entry through NameTagDataBank division/modulo by 19, zeroes vs_kos and play_time_by_fighter, resets sd_count via gmMainLib_8015EF30, and sets x1A2 to 5.
Implemented gmMainLib_8015F150(void) and gmMainLib_8015F260(void) reset loops for FighterData and NameTagData statistics, including fighter_kos, vs_kos, sd_count, play_time_by_fighter, and x1A2.

Added static u8 gmMainLib_804D3EE4 and implemented gmMainLib_8015F600(int,int): one branch clears fighter/save-related data and calls unlock/language/power-count routines; the other initializes a 19-entry nametag bank, copies names from mnName_8023749C when present, writes the terminator byte, and marks x1A1.
Changed gmMainLib_8015FA34 from int to s32, added PAD_STACK(50), wrapped it in #pragma inline_depth(3), changed an assignment from -1 to 0xFF, and removed a stale inline-related todo comment.
Updated gmmain_lib.h prototypes for gmMainLib_8015DBF4, gmMainLib_8015EA80, gmMainLib_8015F150, gmMainLib_8015F260, and gmMainLib_8015FA34.
smart_moves: Reused gmMainLib_8015EF30 for sd_count-like substructures instead of duplicating the reset code, while preserving the casts needed by the current struct state.
Kept nametag index adjustment inline with macros rather than introducing helper calls, which is useful when repeated original code must match without changing call structure.
Preserved exact byte/index behavior with u8 casts, s8 comparisons, and explicit sentinels such as 0x78 and 0xFF.
Used division/modulo by 19 for NameTagDataBank indexing, matching the banked layout visible in the diff.
Scoped the #pragma inline_depth(3) change tightly around gmMainLib_8015FA34, limiting compiler-codegen side effects to the function being matched.
Converted unknown header declarations to concrete signatures only where the implementation evidence made return types and parameters clear.
decomp_lessons: For persistent-name and stat reset routines, exact loop bounds and index casts matter: this diff repeatedly preserves 19, 25, 120, and 6-entry loops with u8 truncation.
Near-matches can require small type and constant-expression changes: gmMainLib_8015FA34 reached 100% after s32 typing, PAD_STACK, inline-depth control, and 0xFF instead of -1.
When a repeated field operation must compile as inline code, local macros can document the behavior while avoiding a new helper function that would not match.

Leave fields as offset-style names such as x2FF8, x1F2C, x1A2, unk_590, and unk_1490 until stronger semantic evidence exists; use known names like vs_kos, sd_count, play_time_by_fighter, handicap, and namedata where the project already has them.
Header cleanup is part of matching work: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with s32 or void improves downstream call-site typing and documents known ABI facts.
A blank PR body and no human review comments means future readers should rely mainly on the diff and bot match report for intent.
naming_conventions: Kept the descriptive name InitializePersistentNameData for address 15EF84 while other still-anonymous routines remain address-based gmMainLib_8015xxxx names.
Used project fixed-width aliases such as s32, u8, and s8 rather than plain int where matching or ABI evidence required it.
Global/static symbols follow the address-based gmMainLib_804Dxxxx pattern, as in gmMainLib_804D3EE4 and gmMainLib_804D3EE0.
Local macro names ADJ_NAMETAG_78 and ADJ_VMD describe the repeated nametag/VsModeData adjustment behavior and the 0x78 sentinel.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(16), PAD_STACK(8), and PAD_STACK(50) to reproduce stack frame shape.
Used #pragma push, #pragma inline_depth(3), and #pragma pop around gmMainLib_8015FA34 to force the desired inlining behavior locally.
Changed gmMainLib_8015FA34's x186C assignment from -1 to 0xFF, matching unsigned-byte storage semantics.
Preserved do-while loops in gmMainLib_8015F600 and exact loop bounds for generated control flow.
Used casts like (u8) arg0, (u8) i, (s8)(u8) *src, and (s32)(s16) un_80305058(...) to control extension/truncation.
Used local macros for repeated ADJ_NAMETAG_78/ADJ_VMD updates so the logic stays inline across many fields.
review_feedback: No human PR body, review comments, or reviews were present in the slice.
decomp-dev bot report for GALE01: matched code 63.83% (+0.03%, +1284 bytes) and matched data 38.25% (+0.00%, +8 bytes).
Bot reported 4 new matches: gmMainLib_8015F260 (+516 bytes), InitializePersistentNameData (+460 bytes), .sdata2 (+8 bytes), and gmMainLib_8015FA34 (+6 bytes from 98.30% to 100.00%).
Bot reported 4 partial improvements: gmMainLib_8015DBF4 to 59.65%, gmMainLib_8015F600 to 86.01%, gmMainLib_8015EA80 to 89.38%, and gmMainLib_8015F150 to 76.97%.
follow_up_queries: Finish matching gmMainLib_8015DBF4, which bot reported at 59.65% after this PR.
Finish matching gmMainLib_8015F600, which bot reported at 86.01% after this PR.
Finish matching gmMainLib_8015EA80 and gmMainLib_8015F150, reported at 89.38% and 76.97%.
Investigate stronger struct names for gmMainLib_804D3EE0->thing.x2FF8, x1F2C, x1CD0, x1CB0, and the repeated VsModeData slots.
Verify the repeated gmMainLib_804D3EE0->unk_1490 adjustment before any cleanup or macro refactor; do not remove it without matching evidence.
Trace gmMainLib_804D3EE4's role as the namedata terminator and its relation to the bot-reported .sdata2 match.
